# simplequad

> TypeScript quadtree with SAT collision detection and MTV output. Zero dependencies. Built for 2D canvas games and interactive apps.

simplequad combines quadtree spatial indexing, Separating Axis Theorem (SAT) intersection testing, and Minimum Translation Vector (MTV) output in a single zero-dependency TypeScript package. One `query()` call returns both the colliding objects and the overlap vectors needed to push them apart — no second library, no glue code.

Supported shapes: BoundingBox (AABB), Circle, Point — freely mixed in the same tree.

## Installation

```
npm install simplequad
```

Or via CDN (UMD global `SimpleQuad`):

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/simplequad@latest/dist/simplequad.umd.js"></script>
```

## Creating a tree

`create` is an alias for `createQuadTree` — both are exported and identical.

**Standard form** — T must extend `Bound` (has `x`, `y`, and `width`/`height` or `r`):

```typescript
import { createQuadTree } from 'simplequad';

const tree = createQuadTree<Entity>(
  { x: 0, y: 0, width: 800, height: 600 },
  { capacity: 5, maxDepth: 8 }  // options are optional
);
```

**Extractor form** — use when your objects don't embed `x`/`y` directly:

```typescript
import { create } from 'simplequad';

interface Sprite {
  id: string;
  pos: { x: number; y: number };
  w: number;
  h: number;
}

const tree = create<Sprite>(
  { x: 0, y: 0, width: 800, height: 600 },
  {
    extractor: (sprite) => ({
      x: sprite.pos.x,
      y: sprite.pos.y,
      width: sprite.w,
      height: sprite.h,
    }),
  }
);
```

When using an extractor, `T` can be any type. `query()` takes a `Bound`, not a `T` — use `queryObject()` to pass a `T` directly.

> Legacy positional form `createQuadTree(bounds, capacity, maxDepth)` is still supported but not preferred.

## QuadTree\<T\> — full API

### Mutation

```typescript
tree.add(object: T): boolean
```
Adds an object. Returns `false` if it falls outside the tree's bounds. Subdivides the containing node when capacity is reached.

```typescript
tree.remove(object: T): boolean
```
Removes an object by reference equality. Returns `false` if not found. O(1) per call regardless of tree size.

```typescript
tree.clear(): void
```
Removes all objects and resets all subdivisions.

```typescript
tree.move(object: T, updateFn: (obj: T) => void): boolean
```
Atomically removes, calls `updateFn` to mutate, then re-inserts. Returns `false` if the object was not in the tree. Use this instead of manual `remove` → mutate → `add` to avoid leaving stale entries.

### Querying

```typescript
tree.query(bounds: Bound): Array<QueryResult<T>>
```
Returns all objects whose bounds intersect `bounds`, with MTV for each. If the same object reference was added to the tree and passed as `bounds`, it is automatically excluded. When using an extractor, filter self manually or use `queryObject()`.

```typescript
tree.queryBroad(bounds: Bound): T[]
```
Like `query()` but skips SAT — returns the objects themselves (no MTV) whose bounds broadly overlap `bounds`. Faster; use for AI sight ranges, audio zones, spawn checks, and flocking neighbor lookups.

```typescript
tree.queryFor(object: T): Array<QueryResult<T>>
```
Like `query()` but takes a `T` instead of a raw `Bound`. When an extractor is configured, applies it automatically and excludes `object` from results by reference. When no extractor is provided, behaves identically to `query(object)`. Prefer this over `query()` when querying for an entity's own collisions.

### Inspection

```typescript
tree.getData(): T[]
```
Returns all objects currently in the tree as a flat deduplicated array.

```typescript
tree.contains(object: T): boolean
```
Returns `true` if the given object reference is in the tree. O(log n) — traverses only the nodes overlapping the object's bounds.

### Structural properties

```typescript
tree.bounds: Readonly<BoundingBox>
tree.capacity: number
tree.maxDepth: number
tree.data: Readonly<T[]>         // objects at this leaf node
tree.quadrants: Readonly<QuadTree<T>[]>  // 4 children (container) or empty (leaf)
```

## Types

```typescript
export interface QuadTreeOptions<T = Bound> {
  capacity?: number;   // default: 5
  maxDepth?: number;   // default: 8
  extractor?: (obj: T) => Bound;
}

export type Bound = BoundingBox | Circle | Point;

export interface Point { x: number; y: number; }
export interface BoundingBox extends Point { width: number; height: number; }
export interface Circle extends Point { r: number; }

export interface QueryResult<T> {
  object: T;
  mtv: MinimumTranslationVectorInfo;
}

export type QueryBroadResult<T> = Omit<QueryResult<T>, 'mtv'>;

export interface MinimumTranslationVectorInfo {
  vector: Point;     // apply to querying object's position to resolve overlap
  direction: Point;  // unit vector pointing from found object toward querying object
  magnitude: number; // overlap depth
}
```

The MTV points from the found object toward the querying object. Apply `mtv.vector` to the querying object's position to push it out of the overlap. Coordinate space: x increases left-to-right, y increases top-to-bottom.

## Game loop pattern (recommended for moving objects)

```typescript
import { createQuadTree } from 'simplequad';

const tree = createQuadTree({ x: 0, y: 0, width: 800, height: 600 });

function gameLoop(entities: Entity[]) {
  tree.clear();
  for (const entity of entities) {
    tree.add(entity);
  }
  for (const entity of entities) {
    const hits = tree.query(entity);
    for (const { object, mtv } of hits) {
      entity.x += mtv.vector.x;
      entity.y += mtv.vector.y;
    }
  }
}
```

## Moving a single object

```typescript
tree.move(entity, (e) => {
  e.x = newX;
  e.y = newY;
});
```

## Debug overlay

`drawDebug` renders the quadtree node boundaries onto a canvas for debugging. It is exported from the main entry point and does not add overhead when unused.

```typescript
import { createQuadTree, drawDebug } from 'simplequad';

// inside your render loop:
drawDebug(tree, ctx);

// with options:
drawDebug(tree, ctx, {
  strokeStyle: 'rgba(0, 255, 0, 0.5)',  // default
  lineWidth: 1,                          // default
  fillStyle: 'rgba(0, 0, 255, 0.05)',   // leaf fill (off by default)
  showDepth: false,                      // label each node with depth
});
```

```typescript
export interface DrawDebugOptions {
  strokeStyle?: string;
  lineWidth?: number;
  fillStyle?: string;
  showDepth?: boolean;
}
```

`drawDebug` saves and restores canvas state so it does not interfere with your drawing.

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/simplequad
- GitHub: https://github.com/rcasto/simplequad
- Live examples: https://rcasto.github.io/simplequad/
- Source (TypeScript): https://github.com/rcasto/simplequad/tree/master/src
